notes:
After configuring (see below) videos play well, BUT switching window (alt+tab) or changing the screen brightness, volume or something similar which shows an OSD (notification drawn on top of active windows) completely breaks the game window (it stops responding to clicks or keypresses). If you can completely disable and OSDs I guess it may be safe enough.

CONFIGURATION (As previously mentioned): Replace TacticsLauncher.exe with BOS_HR.exe (BOS.exe works too, but you can only use this on old screens with low resolutions). Then launch and skip and videos (press Esc). Go to Options -> Display and pick your native resolution and 32bit (these options are not in order, but my native resolution was the first one).
title: Fallout Tactics: Brotherhood of Steel

notes: With Proton 4.2-3 it's no longer necessary to rename files - game runs out of the box. To fix choppy movies change resolution and select 32-bit mode (as mentioned above). Alt-tab works without problems. Steam overlay does not work at all. When notifications happen (e.g. when changing volume in OS), space button stops working - mouse input and other keys on keyboard still work (e.g. you can run by pressing shift and double click) - so game is playable. Space fixes itself after loading save, restarting map, etc.
title: Fallout Tactics: Brotherhood of Steel

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box. Choppy scenes can be fixed by setting resolution to your (native resolution) + (x 32). For example : 1366 x 768 x 32 is the setting I use.
title: Fallout Tactics: Brotherhood of Steel

notes: Rename BOS.exe to TacticsLauncher.exe. first cinematic will be glitchy just skip it. its because of your monitor resolution. in the game menu go to options and edit resolution according to your display. a restart of game maybe required. and you are good to go.
title: Fallout Tactics: Brotherhood of Steel
